title: CaptionTrack
description: Single caption track entry from YouTube's ytInitialPlayerResponse.captions.playerCaptionsTracklistRenderer.captionTracks[] array. Versioned contract for the YouTube-direct provider introduced in v0.3.0.

baseUrl.description: Absolute HTTPS URL pointing at the timedtext payload. May carry a `&sig=` or `&signature=` query parameter that requires deciphering through the player.js operations table; may carry an `&n=` parameter that requires the ncode transformation. The baseUrl is fetched with `&fmt=json3` appended by the provider to obtain a parseable JSON3 response.

languageCode.description: BCP 47 language tag identifying the language of the track. Examples: `en`, `pt`, `pt-BR`, `es-419`. The provider normalises user-supplied `--lang` to the primary subtag before matching against this field.

name.description: Human-readable label that YouTube shows in the captions menu. Typically a short phrase in the track's own language, for example `English` or `Portuguese (auto-generated)`.

vssId.description: Video-specific stream identifier. Either a `.` prefix plus language code (manual tracks, e.g. `.en`) or `a.<lang>` for ASR / auto-generated tracks (e.g. `a.pt`). The provider inspects this field as a secondary signal when `kind` alone is ambiguous.

kind.description: Empty string marks a manual track uploaded by the video owner. The literal `asr` marks a track that was auto-generated by YouTube's speech recognition pipeline. The provider uses this field to honour the `--asr` flag and to prefer manual over auto-generated when the user did not request ASR explicitly.
